rule: Never open with "why didn't you just…" —
why: Combination of B's genuine-vs-rhetorical-question rule and propose-alternative rule covers the anti-pattern and the fix

rule: A blocking objection must carry an alternative or
why: B requires proposing a fix/alternative and stating concrete failure scenario instead of a bare 'this is wrong'

rule: Name what kind of comment it is in
why: B's explicit severity tagging and taste-vs-hard-requirement rules require the same disambiguation

rule: Never rewrite the author's function wholesale inside a
why: B has nothing constraining comment scope to minimal line ranges or barring wholesale rewrites in comments

rule: After three back-and-forths on one thread, take it
why: B requires resolution conditions but never specifies a back-and-forth threshold triggering escalation to a call

rule: Praise specifically ("this made the retry path testable"),
why: B explicitly requires specific, non-obvious praise over generic approval
